one_time_description: Pêches et Océans Canada - Accès aux activités halieutiques (deferred revenue for fishing access rights)
economic_notes: 55% of revenue from government grants (41% in 2020), 33% from fishing revenues (43% in 2020). Subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc., Érablière Malécite S.E.C., Société de gestion Toku. Investments in Les Crevettiers Marinard inc., 9375-8795 Québec inc. (100%), Énergie Éolienne Bas-Saint-Laurent SENC (10%), 9060-7375 Québec inc. (16.67%), Salaweg inc. (33%), Érablière Lac de L'île inc. (50%). Fishing rights valued at 2,388,086. Commercial activities include shrimp and crab fishing, maple sugar bush.
notes: Financial statements are in French. Fiscal year end March 31, 2021. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc., Érablière Malécite S.E.C., Société de gestion Toku. A very large deferred revenue item of $6,133,230 relates to Pêches et Océans Canada fishing access rights (shown on balance sheet as term deposit in trust and deferred revenue). Prior year restatement of $27,675 due to retroactive modification of deferred revenues for Pêches et Océans Canada projects. No schedule of remuneration provided. ISC funding identified as AANC (Affaires Autochtones et du Nord Canada) = $1,821,657. Total government transfers per note 4 = $4,222,218. OSR calculated as total revenue $7,695,364 minus government transfers $4,222,218 = $3,473,146 (primarily commercial/fishing activities). Expenses by segment available but housing not separately identified. Development economic expenses = $1,084,219. Community services = $397,547.
